title: Modular Bar Wine Grid Hutch
summary: A hutch unit for a modular bar system, built around a removable interlocking plywood wine grid that slides out through the front.
description:
This hutch is designed to sit on top of a matching modular base, and its sides are ripped from 3/4" plywood into 11-1/2"-wide strips rather than built from solid 1x12 — plywood strips stay flatter over time and let you share material with the base if you're building both. If you'd rather use real 1x12 lumber, that works too, but measure your actual board width first since it will shift the other measurements slightly.

The wine grid itself is built from interlocking 1/4" plywood dividers — three tall pieces and seven short ones, slid together like a puzzle rather than glued into a fixed frame. Clamping the pieces together and cutting them as a stack, rather than one at a time, is what keeps every slot lining up. The grid is built to lift out through the front for cleaning or rearranging, and the whole hutch must be secured to the base below it and to a wall stud behind — a tall, front-loaded cabinet like this is a genuine tipping hazard if left to stand on its own.

tools.note: Rips the 3/4" plywood into 11-1/2" strips and cuts the stacked wine-grid dividers.
Clamps the wine-grid dividers together for a stacked cut.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, half sheet, ripped into 11-1/2" strips
materials.note: Or substitute solid 1x12 — measure your actual board width and adjust measurements.

cutList.note: Clamp all three together and cut as a stack for identical slots.
Clamp together and cut as a stack.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes and trim the sides
steps.body:
This project is shown built from 3/4" plywood ripped into 11-1/2"-wide strips; you can use solid 1x12 instead, but measure your actual board width first and adjust measurements accordingly.

Drill pocket holes along the side and top edges of both side pieces for attaching the top and legs in later steps. Then, on the outside faces, nail 1x2 trim at the top and bottom with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, keeping the outside edges flush.
materials: 3/4" plywood, half sheet, ripped into 11-1/2" strips

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the sides with 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Build the bottom shelf with pocket holes and attach it to the sides the same way, flush to the bottom.
materials: 3/4" plywood, half sheet, ripped into 11-1/2" strips

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top, flush to the front, along with its trim pieces.
materials: 3/4" plywood, half sheet, ripped into 11-1/2" strips

steps.title: Dry-fit and check square
steps.body: Before cutting the wine grid, dry-fit the carcass together and check it for square — the grid pieces below need a true rectangular opening to slide into.

steps.title: Cut the tall wine-grid dividers
steps.body: Clamp the plywood pieces for the tall dividers together and cut them all at once as a stack — this keeps every slot lining up between pieces. You need three tall dividers.

steps.title: Cut the short wine-grid dividers
steps.body: Cut the seven short dividers the same way, clamped together as a stack.

steps.title: Assemble the wine grid
steps.body: Slide the tall and short dividers together to form the interlocking wine grid.

steps.title: Install the wine grid
steps.body: Insert the assembled grid into the hutch. To make it easy to remove later, keep the small shelf pieces removable from the front — you can then pull the shelves out, collapse the tall dividers, and slide the whole grid out through the front.

steps.title: Attach the back and secure the hutch
steps.body: Nail the back panel on with glue. Secure the hutch to its base to avoid a tipping hazard, and also screw it to a wall stud behind it — this is a tall, front-loaded piece and must not be left freestanding.

images.alt: Modular bar hutch with a removable interlocking wine grid
